title: Weapon Attack
excerpt: A weapon attack is an attack resolved through a weapon or a weapon-like attack profile. It is distinct from a spell attack and should not be treated as the same rules term as an unarmed strike.
markdown:
# Weapon Attack

A weapon attack is an attack that resolves through a weapon's attack profile. It can be melee or ranged, but it remains part of the weapon-attack rules family rather than the [[magical_effect]] or spell-attack family.

## Core Meaning

### Uses a Weapon Profile
A weapon attack relies on the attack and damage profile of a weapon, an equipped weapon state, or another rule that explicitly instructs the system to resolve the attack as a weapon attack.

### Distinct From Spell Attacks
A weapon attack is not the same rules term as a spell attack. Even if a feature has magical qualities, it does not become a spell attack unless a rule explicitly assigns it to that attack family.

### Distinct From Unarmed Strikes
An [[unarmed_strike]] can count as a melee weapon attack for some rules interactions, but it is still not itself a weapon. That distinction matters for rules that require an actual weapon to be used, equipped, carried, drawn, or affected.

## Resolution Context

Weapon attacks are commonly used through the [[attack_action]], opportunity attacks, and other combat features that call for melee or ranged weapon-based attacks. The exact attack modifier, range, damage, and trait interactions come from the weapon profile and any active rules modifying it.

## Practical Meaning

Weapon attack is a rules classification used to determine which attack family is resolving. It helps separate weapon-based combat from spell-based attacks and from body-based strikes, even when those systems can overlap in narrower rule interactions.
